2) silty alluvial marsh transitional facies with a methane-dominant biogenic gas assemblage (methane greater than seventy percent by volume) and a summer-desiccated epistratum with salt crust formation  
3) carbonate-siliciclastic tidal flat veneer with a mixed CO2–CH4 biogenic gas assemblage (CO2 greater than fifty percent by volume) and a winter-saturated supratidal layer regime

Question:
What is the highest pyrogenic carbon stock value in kg.dm-3 for sediments with macroalgal detrital dominance including sporadic silt inclusion, measured at hyperspectral band 1, with moderate allochthonous litter input dominated by deciduous leaf fall seasonality, and a moderate euphotic depth corresponding to mesotrophic conditions?

Restrict the calculation to:

1) elevated shear with sorted bedload transport signatures
2) moderate shear sustaining fine-sand mobility combined with moderate rugosity and interspersed microchannels
